text: The impact is two weeks on the go-live.

Could we move the go-live to July 5th?
Would it be possible to add one week?
If we cut the reports, we can keep June 20th.
I can commit to July 5th.
Sophia, June 20th is not realistic anymore. I want to show you two options.
Diogo, the client has June 20th in the contract. Two options, quickly.
Option A: the full scope, with a two-week extension. Option B: if we cut the custom reports, we keep June 20th.
Two weeks is too much. I can give you one.
One week works, but then I have to push back on the reports. That is the trade-off.
Which reports, exactly? And what does the client lose?
The three custom reports. They lose nothing on day one. We deliver them in August.
That sounds reasonable. One week, reports in August. Can you commit to that?
Yes. I can commit to June 27th, with a three-day buffer. I will send the new plan today.
June 20th is not realistic anymore.
The trade-off is time for scope.

Diogo, I know what you are going to ask me, and I will save you some time. Do not come to me and say that you need more time. That is a problem, and it is not my problem. Come with two options. Option one: a new date that is realistic, and that you can commit to. Option two: the same date, with less scope, and tell me exactly what we cut. Then I can choose, and I can defend the choice with the client. The client will accept a trade. The client will never accept a surprise. And Diogo, one more thing. Whatever we choose, I need a buffer before the go-live.

Thank you for the ten minutes. I will be direct. June 20th is not realistic anymore. The reason is simple: the team does not have the bandwidth for the three custom reports. So I have two options for you. Option A: we keep the full scope, and we move the go-live to July 5th. Option B: we keep June 20th, and we cut the three custom reports, which we deliver in August. In both cases I want a three-day buffer before the go-live. The trade-off is time for scope, and it is your call. Whichever option you choose, I can commit to it, and I will send you the new plan the same day. I need the decision by Friday.

Diogo says that June 20th is not realistic anymore. He presents two options: a new date, or the same date with less scope. Sophia refuses two weeks and offers one. Diogo accepts one week and pushes back on the reports. Finally, he commits to June 27th with a three-day buffer.
